title: Making Instructions Executable
summary: Project instructions become stronger when their observable consequences are encoded as tests. The pattern turns agent guidance into executable contracts without pretending that judgment itself can be fully automated.

title: Operating model for low-Volume High-Software-Content Products
summary: A low-volume high-software-content product cannot be run economically for long on an operating model designed for high-volume software. The fixed costs, team topology, and measurement regime only pay back when there is enough meaningful product change to amortise them.
